metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5477
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2: ASIC2a associates with PSD-95 through its PDZ-binding domain.

Figure 3: PSD-95 increases ASIC2a in spines.

Figure 5: Endogenous ASIC2 enriches in synaptosomes and coimmunoprecipitates with ASIC1.

Figure 8: Eliminating ASIC2 decreases the density of dendritic spines. "We found that disrupting the ASIC2 gene reduced the density of synapses (Fig. 8A,B). The reduction was primarily due to a decrease in spine synapses, with no effect on the density of shaft synapses.". Experimental description: "Representative Western blots of total lysate (L) and synaptosomal fraction (S) of whole brain from wild-type and Myc-ASIC2a transgenic mice."
